mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding real economic activity and rising corporate earnings, which directly supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during rising INDPRO periods are not statistically above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% within three months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the average excess return over the unconditional mean is not negative at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with a very low federal funds rate indicates that monetary policy is unable to stimulate the housing market, signaling structural weakness that depresses economic growth and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal's rank IC over any 3-year window is below 0.02, or the direction of the relationship between HOUST declines and returns is positive in more than 50% of overlapping periods.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains below 5% and payroll employment continues to grow, the economy is in the late-cycle expansion phase where overheating risks and tightening financial conditions lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the short term.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS 3-month growth positive)
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods satisfying the regime condition, or the average excess return over the unconditional mean is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects robust aggregate demand that boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations, providing a persistent positive tailwind for S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.05, or the correlation is not positive at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, the Fed has entered an aggressive easing stance that historically compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns during triggered periods are negative on average, or the positive hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a very low federal funds rate implies negative real interest rates, which historically stimulates risk-taking and equity returns over a quarterly horizon as investors seek real returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between negative real rate signal and 60-day returns is not positive with a t-stat > 2.0, or the rolling rank IC over 3-year windows is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month consecutive decline in housing starts, while unemployment remains low, signals a structural housing downturn that has not yet fully propagated to employment, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as the lagged impact hits growth.
regime_hint: housing downturn with resilient labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a three-month housing start decline, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
